story.text: As a session, I arrived to a DIRTY viewport (bdo's primary tree, uncommitted edits + untracked strays, behind origin/main) and discovered I could not sort it at all: the workstation fence (done-lines 0145/0147) forbids every working-state git verb in the viewport, and the git pen refuses to commit on the trunk, so the dirty-viewport sort model (done-line 0031: commit+push on a branch, then sync) names a remedy a session cannot perform. git.py sync refuses while dirty and points back at the impossible remedy. Deadlock, dogfooded (issue #415). A rule that forces offloading is a bug in the rule. I want the missing sanctioned recovery: a :whiteout utensil on the git pen that preserves the whole pile on a rescue branch BEFORE it cleans, then fast-forwards the now-clean viewport to origin/main and surfaces the rescue branch. bdo confirmed both the direction and the name (#415, via the ask): every workstation ships with :whiteout, proof-carrying like the phrasing door.

briefing.headline: the :whiteout utensil - a session recovers a dirty viewport without losing a byte
briefing.value: git.py grows a `whiteout` verb (and an explicit `sync` on a dirty tree) that sorts a DIRTY viewport, proof-carrying: it preserves the ENTIRE pile (tracked edits + untracked files, `add -A` total snapshot) on a fresh claude/rescue-viewport-<date> branch - committed and pushed - BEFORE it cleans, then returns the viewport to the trunk and fast-forwards to origin/main, and surfaces the rescue branch for reconciliation. The pen is the one actor sanctioned to flip the viewport (it already runs checkout + merge there via subprocess), so a session never routes around the workstation fence. In ambient `sync --hook` the pen surfaces a pointer to the utensil rather than branching and pushing autonomously at every dirty session start.
briefing.why_now: Filed and direction-confirmed by bdo as #415, dogfooded by a viewport-started session that hit the deadlock live and could not land the fix from inside it. It blocks the actual janitorial work bdo asked for: the viewport is dirty and stuck, and there is no sanctioned path to clean it. The fence siblings (workstation-fence, workstation-write-fence, trespass-shame, all epic.substrate) closed the doors; this is their recovery counterpart - teeth without a recovery reflex is a trap.
briefing.if_accepted: A dirty viewport is recoverable through one sanctioned verb that loses nothing; the deadlock #415 names is gone. Every session that arrives to a dirty primary tree (or, in a later increment, any bench in a bad state) reaches for the same named utensil.
briefing.if_rejected: The deadlock stands: a dirty viewport can only be sorted by routing around the fence (forbidden) or by handing bdo the cleanup (the offloading the hard rule forbids). The viewport stays stuck.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and bounded, and the whiteout shape makes it safe by construction: recovery PRESERVES before it cleans, so the worst case loses nothing - the pile is committed and pushed on the rescue branch before the tree is touched, and an offline push leaves it committed locally (still safe). The change is one git-pen verb + a pure naming helper + its test; reverting is deleting them. The §10 tooth is non-vacuous: a control test proves a naive force-clean WOULD lose the pile, so the preservation assertion is load-bearing.
briefing.mechanism: recover_dirty_viewport(viewport, git, emit, bail, ns) creates the rescue branch (rescue_branch_name folds the date + existing refs, never clobbering an earlier same-day rescue), `git add -A` snapshots the whole pile, commits a proof-carrying message naming what is preserved, pushes -u to origin, returns to the trunk (now clean), and `merge --ff-only origin/main`. If nothing is staged (clean tree or a race) it abandons the empty rescue branch and is a plain sync. cmd_sync calls it when the viewport is dirty and carries no local commits origin lacks; in --hook mode it instead surfaces a one-line pointer to the whiteout verb. tests/test_git_whiteout.py builds a real temp repo (bare origin, viewport clone one behind), dirties it, runs the actuator, and asserts the viewport is clean + fast-forwarded to origin/main AND the rescue branch holds the exact pile byte-for-byte (tracked edit + untracked file) AND was pushed; a control proves a naive reset+clean loses that pile; the pure naming fold and the clean-viewport plain-sync path are covered too. Done-line 0170.
